calendar.matters:
FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-08-2014 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT
Off Calendar Oct-09-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Shaun Carberry for and with (trustee) Sherman Yee. Brian McCaffrey for (beneficiaries) Cherlyn Oto & Suzi Mock.

Mr. Carberry stated that the co-trustees were entitled to $30,000.00 each, that Mr. Yee is willing to defer his but would like his deceased brothers share to be distributed to his estate for the benefit of the beneficiaries of that estate as well as to be able to close out that estate. Mr. McCaffrey stated that his clients want all of the fees deferred, would like to update the property to be able to sell at a maximum price and objected to Mr. Yee advancing funds to the trustee. Mr. Yee reported that he has spoken with the brokers for the property, it is not recommended that anything substantial be done to the property as the current market calls for it to be as is, the property is not currently up for sale and stated that he has advanced fees on various occasions and as he is also a beneficiary would not do anything to endanger the trust. Mr. McCaffrey stated that the monthly expenses exceed the monthly income and that the trust has been insolvent on previous occasions. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alavarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Choi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT

Off Calendar Dec-18-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Shaun Carberry for Sherman Yee.

Mr. Carberry stated that he filed the accounting this morning and that the bond was converted from a Conservatorship bond to a Trust bond. Paul Fazio (Bond Agent) was also present and stated that he waives notice on the accounting petition. The matters were taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-18-2012 Off Calendar

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-21-2012 Continued To Dec-18-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Shaun Carberry for and with Sherman Yee (Trustee).

Mr. Carberry stated that he could have the 3rd Account filed in three weeks. The Court asked Counsel to look into the issue of non-payment of the bond premium with regard to the temporary conservatorship. Status on the accounting was continued to December 18, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Garcia.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-21-2012 Continued To Dec-18-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-24-2012 Continued To Nov-21-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Trustee and the Attorney to appear on November 21, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the Third Account has not been filed. Status on the accounting was also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2
Off Calendar Jun-27-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James McCarthy for and with Sherman Yee.

Mr. Carthy stated that the Trustees were paying out of pocket and that they were seeking an allowance to defer these fees at such time when there are enough assets. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
